response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings an impressive multi-decade technical career with genuine developer tooling contributions (test frameworks, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP generators) and strong knowledge-sharing habits via workshops and talks. However, this is explicitly a full-time people-leadership role, and the resume contains no evidence of direct people management — no mention of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or building/growing a team. The role's minimum requirements include 2 years of people management, and the preferred profile asks for 3 years of leading organizational change; neither is addressed anywhere in the resume. Without that management track record, the candidate is mismatched for the role regardless of technical depth.
strengths: Long and broad technical career with genuine developer tooling experience: built open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), set up CI/CD infrastructure, and contributes to MCP/agentic tooling at re:cinq

Demonstrated knowledge-sharing culture through organized Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions, multi-day Selenium courses, and public conference talks — a strong signal for team development instincts
Hands-on experience with testing philosophy and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, pairing) that directly align with the engineering productivity focus of the role
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — no mention of direct reports, hiring, performance management, or team leadership in a formal managerial capacity
Developer tooling work, while present, is largely side-project or incidental to IC roles rather than a sustained primary focus; the role requires 3 years of dedicated developer-tools building
No evidence of strategic roadmap ownership, organizational change leadership, or cross-team stakeholder alignment at scale — all core responsibilities listed in the job description
